summary: Replace `time.Sleep(wait)` in 429 and 5xx retry paths with `select { case <-ctx.Done(): ...; case <-time.After(wait): }` so Ctrl+C is responsive during backoff waits.
reason: Greptile P2 review finding on PR #676 (fifth pass summary): Retry-After backoffs can be tens of seconds; bare time.Sleep ignores cmd.Context() cancellation, so Ctrl+C would not interrupt a long rate-limit wait. The context plumbing from the third-pass patch is already wired into do(); this completes the chain by honouring it during backoff as well as during the in-flight request.
